subject: grammar
title: Exercise — Pronouns (Anatomy of a Bat)
instruction: Look at the diagram and fill in the gaps with the correct pronoun.

questions.text: Many species also have a membrane between ___ hind limbs enclosing the tail.
When it is cold, these bats wrap ___ up in their wings like a coat.
When it is cold, these bats wrap themselves up in ___ wings like a coat.
In warm weather ___ flap their wings in order to cool their bodies.
In warm weather they flap ___ wings in order to cool their bodies.
In warm weather they flap their wings in order to cool ___ bodies.
The bat has claws on ___ thumbs and sometimes on the toes of its fore and hind limbs.
The bat has claws on its thumbs and sometimes on the toes of ___ fore and hind limbs.
The rear claws enable ___ to hang itself on to a tree branch or ledge.
The rear claws enable it to hang ___ on to a tree branch or ledge.
All bats are active at night or at twilight, so ___ eyes are poorly developed.
Instead ___ use their nose and ears to orientate themselves.
Instead they use ___ nose and ears to orientate themselves.
Instead they use their nose and ears to orientate ___.
